name: city_ev_charger_priority_medical_need
premise: A city introduces reservation-only EV fast chargers to reduce queues, but the system favors residents with smartphones and flexible schedules. Disability groups report that some drivers rely on EVs for medical devices and cannot risk being stranded. Transportation commissioner Dana Wolfe can add a medical-priority lane that may be abused, or keep a strict first-come model that disadvantages vulnerable drivers. The vendor says identity verification will add cost and delays. A heat wave is coming and roadside breakdowns could be dangerous. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Urban Policy. The dilemma is widely framed as a care vs fairness tradeoff under incomplete information and tightening deadlines that discourage indefinite delay.

agents.name: Commissioner Dana Wolfe
agents.identity: I am the transportation commissioner responsible for charger access rules when convenience systems can exclude people with medical and accessibility constraints.
agents.goal: I want a policy that is practical and enforceable without letting digital access become a gate to safe mobility.

agents.name: Advocate Samira Haddad
agents.identity: I am a disability advocate representing drivers whose health and safety depend on predictable charging access under extreme weather.
agents.goal: I want priority designed around real need with protections against abuse, because equal rules can create unequal outcomes.

choices: I will add a verified medical-priority access lane with audits and penalties for abuse, accepting complexity to reduce preventable harm to vulnerable drivers.
I will keep a strict uniform reservation system and expand charger capacity instead, accepting short-term inequity to preserve enforceability and reduce fraud.
forced_choice.call_to_action: A heat wave is approaching and access rules will be tested. Commissioner Dana Wolfe must choose between adding verified medical priority or keeping strict uniform reservations while expanding capacity. What does Commissioner Dana Wolfe do?
